criteria_checked.note: CONFIRMED. tests/fixtures/m8/test-manifest-drift.sh:36 byte-compares `cmp -s $emitf $M8_MANIFEST` where $emitf is dmc-install.sh --emit-manifest (:28,35) and $M8_MANIFEST is the committed INSTALL_MANIFEST.md; it runs under run_m8_suite (bin/dmc:293 loop lists test-manifest-drift.sh), which is in selftest --all (bin/dmc:581) AND the BLOCKING CI step selftest m8-suite (.github/workflows/dmc-ci.yml:178-179, no continue-on-error, unlike the advisory --all replay at :191-194). The manifest half is genuinely + permanently pinned; net-new manifest code is correctly zero.

CONFIRMED genuine gap. dmc-agents-md.py selftest() (bin/lib/dmc-agents-md.py:680-834) exercises only tempfile.mkdtemp synthetic repos (node/py/empty/companion/parity/big) against generator output; it never reads or cmp/diffs the repo own committed AGENTS.md. The m6.5 shell suite test-agents-md.sh drives only mktemp hosts (:69-177) and its one committed-file touch is an unrelated `wc -c` size read (:165). No committed-AGENTS.md byte-equality exists anywhere; the AGENTS.md drift pin is legitimately net-new.

CONFIRMED, stronger than stated. The 802/3/3 aggregate is computed over a HARDCODED 49-tool list ALL_SH_TOOLS = NOFLAG_TOOLS + SELFTEST_TOOLS (dmc-legacy-selftest.py:111-112) vs PINNED_BASELINE (:118), not a glob, so a new tests/fixtures/m6.5/*.sh cannot enter the count. The plan describes a startswith('dmc-v0.') stray-guard; the actual mechanism is a fixed enumeration + a bin/lib<->.harness/evidence mirror, which is strictly stronger (a tests/ file is in neither set). run_m65_suite (bin/dmc:275-285) returns only rc 0/1 with NO pinned count; grep found no exact-inventory guard over tests/fixtures/m6.5, so adding a 4th `for s in` loop entry is safe. CI selftest m65-suite (.github/workflows/dmc-ci.yml:172-173) invokes run_m65_suite and auto-covers the new script with NO workflow edit.

CONFIRMED live + structural + empirical. Baseline both regen-diffs EMPTY at bbeb277 this session, including the suite's EXACT form `bin/dmc agents-md --root <abs> --stdout | diff - AGENTS.md` EMPTY with zero absolute paths leaked (closes Risk-table row 6, the --root . vs --root <abs> question) and `dmc-install.sh --emit-manifest | diff - INSTALL_MANIFEST.md` EMPTY. Structural: INSTALL_MANIFEST references bin/dmc by NAME only (INSTALL_MANIFEST.md:12), no content hash, no tests/fixtures copy rows; AGENTS.md section-4 classifies only package.json/pyproject.toml under tests/fixtures (contract class) so a .sh is not a landmark and section-5 counts are unchanged. E-cycle empirically proved the identical class: dmc-fable-core-e-build-20260710.md:34 records 'agents-md drift empty; m8 manifest-drift 10/0' after adding a hermetic test + editing bin/dmc. The plan correctly carries defensive regenerate-if-drift rows for BOTH artifacts (avoids the one-sided-lockstep forbidden fix class). ADVISORY nit: F5 cites dmc-fable-core-e-runstart.md:103-107 for the precedent, which did not resolve; the real neutrality evidence is dmc-fable-core-e-build-20260710.md:34 — cosmetic.

CONFIRMED as a correctly-specified design requirement (suite does not exist yet). Proposed Changes (3)(4)(5) require negative controls to mutate only mktemp COPIES of AGENTS.md and assertion (5) to use a git status --porcelain before/after DELTA, never git-status-as-a-pass-signal. This mirrors the sibling hermetic suites (test-agents-md.sh:45,199-202 before/after porcelain delta; test-manifest-drift.sh mutates only $M8_WORK copies) and does NOT replicate the frozen V15 anti-pattern (dmc-v0.6.0-verify.sh:147-166 classifies tracked git-status changes as pass/fail). Implementation hermeticity is a verify-gate concern the plan pins via AC Criterion 2 (real-repo porcelain byte-identical before/after).

CONFIRMED. bin/dmc is enforcement-class -> non-degrading FLAG expected (not a blocker), matching the E-cycle (dmc-fable-core-e-build-20260710.md: 'non-degrading landmark FLAG (bin/dmc, MILESTONES); NO G4 override'); the plan sets no DMC_GATE_PROTECTED override and no protected path is in scope. Out of Scope explicitly excludes the generators, PINNED_BASELINE, test-manifest-drift.sh, frozen dmc-v* tools, hooks, schemas, _m65common.sh, and .github/workflows. Acceptance Criteria are explicit + testable and include the deliberate-drift NEGATIVE control (Criterion 2) plus a clean-tree positive --all 802/3/3 run (Criterion 4). Additional compat point: the BLOCKING CI MID-sandwich porcelain check (.github/workflows/dmc-ci.yml:181-189) requires the checkout clean after the m65 suite — the mktemp-confined + read-only-committed-file design satisfies it.

ANSWER (a): m6.5 is the correct home; the m8/m6.5 asymmetry is ACCEPTABLE. Each drift pin lives with its generator family (dmc-install.sh -> m8; dmc-agents-md.py -> m6.5). Re-homing the working manifest test into a unified suite would perturb the m8 suite AND the blocking CI m8-suite step for zero functional gain — a surgical-change / no-out-of-scope-edit issue (Constitution Art. VIII). Locality beats uniformity; the asymmetry is documented in Out-of-Scope + Risks.

ANSWER (b): KEEP the section-delete control — it covers the AGENTS.md all-10-sections completeness invariant that a one-byte flip does not. ADVISORY: to carry the manifest sibling's 'cannot be defeated by deletion because the generator re-emits it' semantics (test-manifest-drift.sh:85-89 compares emit vs a section-deleted copy), the AGENTS.md control should compare the REGEN output against the section-deleted COPY (regen still emits all 10 sections -> compare FAILS), not merely compare two tampered copies. Minor clarification for the executor, not a blocker.

ANSWER (c): WORKING-TREE base is CORRECT (planner's choice). The pin semantic is about-to-commit == generator; the manifest sibling already reads the on-disk committed file (test-manifest-drift.sh:33-36 reads the $M8_MANIFEST path, not git show HEAD:). A HEAD-blob base would false-fail a cycle that legitimately regenerates + stages the artifact before commit, and would also break compatibility with the BLOCKING CI MID-sandwich (.github/workflows/dmc-ci.yml:181-189). Corollary constraint the executor must honor: NEVER regenerate AGENTS.md in place during the suite — read the committed file, write only to mktemp.
